intro: Dortmanna linarioides, also known as Water Stork's Bill, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and inflammatory conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dortmanna linarioides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects.
